summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #245 (ContextMenu) and #249 (MenuBar) as framework-agnostic core state machines in the ars-ui repo, delivered as a single combined PR with full TDD, coverage, mutation, and audit gates.
summary.outcome: Both agnostic cores were implemented with inline tests, per-part snapshots, proptest invariants, and spec updates. PR #679 was opened, passed pre-push CI, received and addressed 7 Codex review findings, and had a follow-up commit pushed. PR is awaiting final Codex approval and CI green.

repo: Snapshot fixtures follow a per-part/per-branch pattern (one `.snap` file per named part or output-affecting branch), not one aggregate snapshot per component — matching the existing `selection/menu` style.

The required workflow sequence is: TDD red→green → `post-implementation-audit` skill (2+ 'anything else missing?' rounds) → `cargo llvm-cov` coverage → `cargo mutants` mutation → `cargo xci-fast` pre-push gate → commit with co-author → non-draft PR → `waiting-for-codex-review` loop until 👍.
GitHub Project #2 ('ars-ui implementation roadmap') tracks issue status; items must be moved to 'In Progress' before implementation begins.
Agnostic core machines use `NoEffect` as the effect type — no live DOM operations; adapters translate intent into mounted-handle work.
PropTest invariants are added as `#[ignore]`d tests and run explicitly with `-- --ignored <test_name>`.
The `i18n` feature flag is required for most ars-components tests and must be passed explicitly: `--features i18n`.
`.cargo/mutants.toml` is used for narrow mutation exclusions; only the non-`std` fallback timestamp path was excluded, justified by the std-enabled mutation profile being unable to observe it.
`cargo xtask spec validate`, `spec toc`, and `spec compile-snippets` are all required spec gates after any spec changes.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label (`gh pr edit <pr> --add-label snapshot-reviewed`) must be applied when `.snap` fixtures change.
Stale `context_menu`/`menu_bar` language in Leptos and Dioxus adapter specs must be updated alongside the component specs in the same PR.

code.finding: The highlight proptest utility module was loaded without the `i18n` feature gate even though all its helpers and the component under test are i18n-gated, causing baseline compile failures for `cargo mutants` (which runs without `--features i18n`). Fixed by adding a matching cfg gate on the module.

The `adjacent_key` helper had a type mismatch: collection neighbor lookups return borrowed keys while the empty-focus branch returned owned keys. Fixed by normalizing the match arms to return owned keys.

Codex review finding: `on_target_keydown` must accept adapter-resolved anchor coordinates (x, y) for both `Shift+F10` and the Context Menu key, not hardcode `(0.0, 0.0)`. The dispatch helper signature was updated to take an optional anchor parameter.

Codex review finding: `SyncProps` disabling an open/active MenuBar must transition to `State::Inactive` and fire close callbacks; `UpdateMenus` removing the active menu must also produce `State::Inactive`. Both transitions were missing and added.

Codex review finding: vertical-orientation MenuBar must route ArrowUp/ArrowDown for trigger traversal (not ArrowLeft/ArrowRight). The content keydown dispatch was using the wrong key variants for vertical layout.

workflow: Using `cargo insta --unreferenced=delete` with a test filter deletes unrelated snapshots from the entire package. Always restore with `git restore --staged :/` and accept only the intended new fixtures manually.
Running two `cargo llvm-cov` commands concurrently causes `llvm-profdata` merge collisions; run coverage runs sequentially with `--output-path` flags pointing to distinct files.

`cargo mutants` can get stuck in its final `cargo test --no-run` compile phase under high concurrent cargo load on the machine; monitor artifact count and be prepared to kill and rely on other verification signals.
When GraphQL queries are embedded in shell heredocs, unbalanced braces in the query string cause silent parse errors — test queries standalone with `gh api graphql` before embedding in polling loops.
Mutation test exclusions should be justified narrowly (e.g. non-`std` fallback paths the profile cannot observe) rather than used to paper over real test gaps.

friction: `cargo mutants` got stuck in the post-review rerun during a final `cargo test --no-run` compile phase, requiring a `kill -9` after several minutes; the post-review MenuBar mutation rerun was abandoned.
Concurrent cargo activity across worktrees on the same machine significantly slowed both mutation runs beyond normal estimates.
`cargo insta --unreferenced=delete` with a module filter deleted unrelated snapshot fixtures; required `git restore --staged :/` to recover before selectively accepting the new per-part snapshots.
Concurrent `cargo llvm-cov` invocations collided on `llvm-profdata` output, requiring sequential reruns with explicit `--output-path` separation.
The initial GraphQL thread-query had a malformed closing brace, causing silent failures; required debugging the query syntax before the Codex review poll loop worked correctly.
open_items: PR #679 is awaiting final Codex 👍 and CI green before merge; issues #245 and #249 will auto-close via PR keywords on merge.

Post-review MenuBar mutation rerun was not completed (process killed due to stall); mutation evidence for the review-fix commit relies on focused tests, proptests, and coverage rather than a completed mutation run.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter components for ContextMenu and MenuBar are explicitly out of scope and deferred to future issues.

Focused line coverage for context_menu is ~94% and menu_bar is ~93%; the remaining uncovered regions (primarily non-`std` fallback paths) are not excluded but are not exercised by the default feature profile.
